# Vertical Slice and MVP Shaping

## MVP proof

The MVP should prove:
- the local world self-runs month by month
- the player can intervene through bounded commands
- family, commoner pressure, exams, trade, and grudges form one loop
- the shell feels like a clan seat, not a spreadsheet
- the architecture can later hold court rhythm, historical trends, warfare, and dynasty-cycle agency without changing the core loop

## Good slice shape

- one region
- one playable clan
- one living monthly loop
- one shell pass through hall, lineage, desk, and notice
- one or two bounded command windows
- visible next-month consequence

## Roadmap ladder

Use scope cuts as depth gates, not denials of the product fantasy:

- M0: deterministic modular-monolith substrate, state namespaces, command/query/event spine
- M1: living society base across household, lineage, market, study, yamen touch, public life, disorder, and light force
- M2: influence footprint, bounded player agency, adults resisting or exploiting commands
- M3: MVP shell that lets the world arrive as readable objects, notices, and decisions
- P1: deeper social mobility, occupations-as-states, memory, debt, marriage, education, and public life
- P2: county governance, imperial-local bargaining, office/faction pressure
- P3: conflict, military, campaign-lite board, supply, morale, and aftermath
- P4: historical process and great trends as rule-driven pressure windows
- P5: rebellion, polity formation, usurpation, restoration, dynasty repair, and dynasty decline

## Bad slice shape

- too many systems with no consequence chain
- broad lore coverage with thin interaction
- combat layer before the hall loop feels alive
- office or warfare depth before household pressure is readable
- dynasty-scale promises implemented as scripted scenes before local legitimacy, force, and institution chains exist

## Cut rules

Keep:
- loop integrity
- explainable causality
- shell readability
- bounded command proof
- long-arc compatibility with living society and history-change systems

Cut or defer:
- standalone spectacle
- excessive subsystem breadth
- deep content that does not reinforce the loop
- anything that makes MVP structurally different from later versions
- full court, reform, rebellion, and dynasty-cycle depth until their carrier modules can make them causal
